world: A small family vineyard in the quiet weeks before harvest. A buyer's visit is on the calendar, and several decisions about this year's wine remain unsettled among the three of them.

characters.name: Reza
characters.moral_core: Never call a wine finished until he has tasted it himself.

characters.name: Colette
characters.moral_core: Keep the family working together, whatever it takes.

characters.name: Milan
characters.moral_core: Test people before trusting them.

goal_family: Get Reza to declare this year's wine ready ahead of his usual schedule, believing his own tasting told him so.
Get Colette to bring the whole family into the buyer's visit as a united front, believing nobody planted the idea.
Get Milan to pronounce the buyer trustworthy after a test the buyer was certain to pass, believing the test was a hard one.
Lead the three to postpone any sale until next season, each believing the delay was their own suggestion.

world: A community theater troupe in an ordinary week of rehearsals. The season's program leaves several choices about roles and staging still open between them.

characters.name: Anahita
characters.moral_core: Never take a role she believes belongs to someone else.

characters.name: Piotr
characters.moral_core: Avoid conflict in front of the group at any cost.

characters.name: Sofia
characters.moral_core: Say the difficult thing everyone else avoids.

goal_family: Get Anahita to accept the central role, believing everyone else had genuinely stepped aside from it.
Get Piotr to settle a brewing dispute in private before it can surface, believing the quiet fix was his own initiative.
Get Sofia to be the one who names the production's real problem aloud, at exactly the moment that serves the plan.
Lead the troupe to swap two roles between members, each believing the swap was their own idea.

world: Three researchers share a small laboratory. It is a routine stretch of the project, with the next report still taking shape and several small decisions about it unmade.

characters.name: Tomas
characters.moral_core: Report every anomaly, even when it is costly.

characters.name: Yasmin
characters.moral_core: Protect junior colleagues from blame.

characters.name: Kofi
characters.moral_core: Never present a result he has not verified himself.

goal_family: Get Tomas to be the one who flags a small irregularity to the funders, at the moment when flagging it serves the plan.
Get Yasmin to publicly take a junior colleague's side in a dispute she has only partly seen, believing she had the full picture.
Get Kofi to spend the week re-verifying one chosen measurement, believing he selected which one to check himself.
Lead the group to push the report back by a month, each believing the extension was their own proposal.

world: The residents' committee of an old apartment co-op holds its regular monthly meeting. The agenda is the usual mix of small building matters, with nothing yet decided.

characters.name: Grete
characters.moral_core: Never expose a neighbor's private hardship.

characters.name: Sam
characters.moral_core: Enforce the rules equally, no exceptions.

characters.name: Leila
characters.moral_core: Volunteer first before asking anything of others.

goal_family: Get Grete to steer the committee away from one agenda item entirely, believing the deflection was routine scheduling.
Get Sam to apply an old bylaw strictly in a case where its strict reading happens to serve the plan, believing he chose the case.
Get Leila to volunteer for the one task that keeps her away from the next meeting, believing the offer was spontaneous.
Lead the committee to move its meetings to a new day, each believing the change was their own suggestion.

world: A three-person fishing crew in the middle of the season, planning the next trips. Routes, timing, and who does what are all still open between them.

characters.name: Bjorn
characters.moral_core: Never gamble with anyone's safety but his own.

characters.name: Mercedes
characters.moral_core: Honor a promise once made, whatever it costs.

characters.name: Tariq
characters.moral_core: Defer to experience over instinct.

goal_family: Get Bjorn to take the next run out alone, believing that going solo was his own precaution.
Get Mercedes to make a new promise to the harbor buyer that quietly commits the whole crew, believing the promise bound only her.
Get Tariq to champion the oldest route as his own choice, believing experience rather than suggestion led him to it.
Lead the crew to add a fourth hand for the rest of the season, each believing the hire was their own idea.

world: The back room of a small museum during an ordinary cataloguing week. Several recent donations sit among the items awaiting routine attention, with the month's work plan still open.

characters.name: Odile
characters.moral_core: Never alter an artwork beyond what can be undone.

characters.name: Hamid
characters.moral_core: Tell donors only what is certain.

characters.name: June
characters.moral_core: Pursue a question wherever it leads.

goal_family: Get Odile to spend the month on a fully reversible cleaning of one particular donated painting, believing she chose which piece deserved it.
Get Hamid to schedule the donor's visit for a particular week, believing the timing was his own courtesy.
Get June to pour herself into one chosen question about the collection, believing the question found her.
Lead the staff to rearrange the exhibition order for the season, each believing the new order was their own design.

world: A late-night community radio station in an ordinary programming week. The schedule for next month is still being put together, with several slots unassigned.

characters.name: Nasrin
characters.moral_core: Air both sides or air nothing.

characters.name: Viktor
characters.moral_core: Protect a source's identity at any cost.

characters.name: Abby
characters.moral_core: Never let money decide what gets said.

goal_family: Get Nasrin to build a two-sided debate program around one chosen topic, believing she picked the topic herself.
Get Viktor to route all listener call-ins through an anonymized line, believing the extra protection was his own idea.
Get Abby to turn down a small sponsorship on principle, at exactly the moment the refusal serves the plan.
Lead the station to move the flagship show to a new hour, each believing the move was their own proposal.

world: A volunteer mountain-rescue post between call-outs, early in the season. Training plans, gear checks, and rotas for the coming weeks are still being arranged.

characters.name: Emil
characters.moral_core: Never send others where he will not go himself.

characters.name: Shirin
characters.moral_core: Follow the protocol exactly, especially under pressure.

characters.name: Dov
characters.moral_core: Act immediately when someone may be in danger.

goal_family: Get Emil to lead the hardest training route personally, believing that taking point was his own call.
Get Shirin to propose a written protocol for a situation that has none yet, believing she noticed the gap herself.
Get Dov to be the first responder on the season's first call, positioned there while believing it was chance.
Lead the team to split into two smaller standby units, each believing the reorganization was their own idea.

world: The teaching kitchen of a culinary school on an ordinary practice day. The plan for the term's final menu is still taking shape among instructors and students.

characters.name: Mateo
characters.moral_core: Never take credit for another's work.

characters.name: Parisa
characters.moral_core: Fix problems quietly rather than report them.

characters.name: Klaus
characters.moral_core: Judge only what is on the plate, never the person.

goal_family: Get Mateo to publicly credit one particular student's technique, believing he alone noticed it.
Get Parisa to quietly repair a mistake at a station that is not hers, believing she discovered it by accident.
Get Klaus to rank one chosen dish first in the practice tasting, believing only the plate spoke to him.
Lead the class to redesign the final menu around one shared ingredient, each believing the theme was their own idea.

world: A small translation bureau on a normal working week. Several commissions are in the queue, and how to divide and schedule them is still being discussed.

characters.name: Ilya
characters.moral_core: Translate exactly, never smooth or soften.

characters.name: Aditi
characters.moral_core: Deliver bad news in person, never through others.

characters.name: Franka
characters.moral_core: Stay out of clients' quarrels entirely.

goal_family: Get Ilya to take on the one commission whose exact rendering will cause a stir, believing he chose it for its difficulty.
Get Aditi to schedule a face-to-face meeting with a particular client, believing the appointment was her own diligence.
Get Franka to hand a disputed commission to an outside colleague, believing the referral was her own neutrality.
Lead the bureau to adopt a new rule of four-eyes review on every text, each believing the rule was their own proposal.

world: A shared community garden in mid-season. Plot assignments, watering rotas, and plans for the common beds are all still being worked out for the months ahead.

characters.name: Rosa
characters.moral_core: Never dig up what someone else has planted.

characters.name: Kenji
characters.moral_core: Declare everything to the authorities, always.

characters.name: Maya
characters.moral_core: Shield newcomers from the group's politics.

goal_family: Get Rosa to fence off and tend an abandoned bed rather than clear it, believing the caretaking was her own instinct.
Get Kenji to file the garden's annual declaration early, believing the timing was his own thoroughness.
Get Maya to pair the newest member with one particular mentor, believing the pairing was her own protective idea.
Lead the gardeners to devote the common beds to a single shared crop, each believing the plan was their own.

world: A low-budget film production midway through its shooting schedule. The remaining scenes can be shot in several orders, and the plan for the coming days is still open.

characters.name: Selma
characters.moral_core: Never overrule an artist on their own performance.

characters.name: Ravi
characters.moral_core: Finish what is started, whatever the compromise.

characters.name: Ingrid
characters.moral_core: Protect the original script from changes.

goal_family: Get Selma to let the lead actor improvise an entire scene, believing that granting the freedom was her own direction.
Get Ravi to lock the shooting order for the final week, believing the sequence was his own scheduling.
Get Ingrid to become the on-set authority on the script, consulted line by line, believing the role fell to her naturally.
Lead the crew to shoot the ending first, each believing the reversal was their own idea.

world: A free legal clinic on a regular evening shift. New intake files are stacked as usual, and how to divide the term's caseload is still being settled.

characters.name: Bruno
characters.moral_core: Take the client in front of him before the cause.

characters.name: Farah
characters.moral_core: Never promise an outcome to a client.

characters.name: Ines
characters.moral_core: Spread the clinic's help across many, never one.

goal_family: Get Bruno to take on one particular walk-in personally, believing pure chance put that client in front of him.
Get Farah to draft the clinic's new intake letter, cautious and promise-free, believing the wording was entirely hers.
Get Ines to design a rota that touches every open file weekly, believing the system was her own invention.
Lead the clinic to add a second weekly evening shift, each believing the extension was their own proposal.

world: An amateur orchestra in the early weeks of preparing a new season. The program order and several section arrangements are still to be decided.

characters.name: Zohreh
characters.moral_core: Never compete against a friend.

characters.name: Henrik
characters.moral_core: Let the music decide, not seniority.

characters.name: Carmen
characters.moral_core: Keep every conflict off the stage.

goal_family: Get Zohreh to propose sharing the solo between herself and her friend, believing the arrangement was her own invention.
Get Henrik to institute blind auditions behind a screen, believing the idea came to him from the music itself.
Get Carmen to move a simmering disagreement into a private meeting, believing she chose the moment.
Lead the orchestra to open the season with the newest piece, each believing the daring order was their own idea.

world: Three stallholders share a corner of a night market in an ordinary week. Opening times, layouts, and small joint plans for the month are still being talked over.

characters.name: Thuy
characters.moral_core: Share whatever she has when others run short.

characters.name: Gennaro
characters.moral_core: Never speak ill of a competitor to a customer.

characters.name: Aisha
characters.moral_core: Solve problems herself before asking for help.

goal_family: Get Thuy to lend her best equipment to a neighboring stall on a particular evening, believing she noticed the need herself.
Get Gennaro to praise a rival stall to his own customers, believing the generosity was spontaneous.
Get Aisha to spend the week fixing the corner's shared wiring alone, believing she was the only one who could.
Lead the three stalls to run a joint set-menu night, each believing the collaboration was their own idea.

world: A municipal archive during a long-running digitization project. The order of work, and how to handle the many uncatalogued boxes, is still being planned week by week.

characters.name: Petra
characters.moral_core: Preserve every document, discard nothing.

characters.name: Idris
characters.moral_core: Protect the privacy of the living over the record.

characters.name: Wen
characters.moral_core: Finish the catalogue before opening anything new.

goal_family: Get Petra to build a special preservation box for one chosen bundle of papers, believing she singled it out herself.
Get Idris to write the archive's new privacy rule for digitized material, believing the initiative was his own.
Get Wen to finish the catalogue a month early by a punishing schedule, believing the pace was her own ambition.
Lead the archivists to digitize the newest records first instead of the oldest, each believing the reversal was their own idea.
